curation_notes: The vulnerability is CVE-2022-4135, a heap buffer overflow in Chromium's GPU command buffer service. Electron cherry-picks the Chromium fix as a patch file (patches/chromium/cherry-pick-6b4af5d82083.patch). The patch replaces ValidForTarget (which uses generic target-based max mip level limits) with ValidForTextureTarget (which queries the actual texture object's mip levels) across multiple GPU command handlers in gles2_cmd_decoder.cc and texture_manager.cc. Only 2 files changed in the electron repo (the patch index and the new patch file), so noisy_patch is false. The location is set to patches/chromium/.patches since the vulnerable code is in the bundled Chromium source managed through electron's patch system, and the fix adds an entry to this patch index. Pre-assigned class buffer-overflow is correct per CWE-787 and advisory description.
